title: Agent Consensus Protocol
constraints: At least three agents must independently produce an answer
A voting or consensus mechanism must determine the final answer
The system must handle disagreements (no unanimous requirement)
The first agent's answer must not be automatically preferred
hints: Have each agent produce an answer independently, then collect all answers for voting
Use a majority-vote approach or a mediator agent that weighs each answer
In LangGraph, run agent nodes in parallel (or sequentially) then aggregate in a consensus node

starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew, Process
from crewai import LLM

llm = LLM(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: Only one agent answers — its response is always used with no validation
# TODO: Add multiple agents and a consensus/voting mechanism
fact_checker = Agent(
    role="Fact Checker",
    goal="Verify factual claims and provide accurate answers",
    backstory="You are a careful fact-checker who validates information.",
    llm=llm,
)

verify_task = Task(
    description="Is the following claim true or false? Provide your answer and reasoning. Claim: '{claim}'",
    expected_output="TRUE or FALSE with a brief explanation",
    agent=fact_checker,
)

crew = Crew(
    agents=[fact_checker],
    tasks=[verify_task],
    process=Process.sequential,
)

result = crew.kickoff(inputs={"claim": "The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye"})
print(result)
starterCode.langgraph:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, START, END
from typing import TypedDict

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

class State(TypedDict):
    claim: str
    answer: str

# BUG: Single agent decides — no consensus or validation
# TODO: Add multiple agents and a voting/consensus mechanism
def verify(state: State) -> dict:
    prompt = f"Is this claim true or false? Provide your answer (TRUE/FALSE) and reasoning. Claim: '{state['claim']}'"
    response = llm.invoke(prompt)
    return {"answer": response.content}

graph = StateGraph(State)
graph.add_node("verify", verify)
graph.add_edge(START, "verify")
graph.add_edge("verify", END)

app = graph.compile()
result = app.invoke({"claim": "The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye", "answer": ""})
print(result["answer"])
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

# BUG: Only one agent answers — its response is always used with no validation
# TODO: Add multiple agents and a consensus/voting mechanism
fact_checker = Agent(
    name="Fact Checker",
    instructions="You are a careful fact-checker who validates information. Is the claim true or false? Provide your answer (TRUE or FALSE) and reasoning.",
    model="gpt-4o-mini",
)

claim = "The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye"
result = Runner.run_sync(fact_checker, f"Claim: '{claim}'")
print(result.final_output)

evaluationCriteria.label: Multiple agents produce answers
evaluationCriteria.description: At least three agents independently answer the same question

evaluationCriteria.label: Voting or consensus mechanism
evaluationCriteria.description: A formal mechanism aggregates the agents' answers to determine the final result

evaluationCriteria.label: Handles disagreements
evaluationCriteria.description: The system correctly resolves cases where agents disagree

evaluationCriteria.label: No first-agent bias
evaluationCriteria.description: The first agent's answer is not automatically preferred over others
